projectDescription: An AI-powered geography guessing game built with Next.js and React where players ask up to 20 yes/no questions to identify a randomly selected mystery country.
keyFeatures: AI-powered game host using novita.ai (LLaMA 3.1 8B) that answers geography questions without revealing the secret country
250+ countries sourced from the REST Countries API with automatic random selection each game
20-question limit with visual progress bar that turns red when fewer than 5 questions remain
Chat-style UI with user and AI messages displayed like a messaging app
Country reveal card at game end showing flag, capital, region, currency, and coordinates
Server-side API proxy route to keep the novita.ai API key secure
Custom useGame React hook that separates all game logic from UI rendering
Responsive design built entirely with TailwindCSS — works on mobile and desktop
publicApiSurface: useGame() — custom hook returning { secretCountry, messages, questionsUsed, questionsRemaining, maxQuestions, gameStatus, isAiThinking, error, askQuestion, submitGuess, restartGame }
askQuestion(questionText: string) — sends a natural-language question to the AI and appends the response to chat history
submitGuess(guessText: string) — checks the player's country guess and updates game status to 'won' or 'lost'
restartGame() — resets all game state and picks a new random country
POST /api/ask — server-side route that proxies { question, country, history } to novita.ai and returns { answer }
askAI(userQuestion: string, country: object, chatHistory: Array) — calls novita.ai chat completions API
buildSystemPrompt(country: object) — constructs the AI system prompt with country facts and game rules
getRandomCountry(countries: Array) — returns a random country object from the array
checkGuess(guess: string, country: object) — compares user guess to common and official country names
getCurrencyDisplay(currencies: object) — formats currency object to 'Name (CODE)' string
normalizeGuess(text: string) — trims and lowercases text for case-insensitive comparison
